name: Kenny Barron
abstract: Kenneth Barron (born June 9, 1943) is an American jazz pianist and composer who has appeared on hundreds of recordings as leader and sideman and is considered one of the most influential mainstream jazz pianists since the bebop era.

interviews.title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Me and Kenny Barron had been talking about doing an album together. We had been playing a lot together... We recorded an album called “You Had Better Listen” in 1967. Kenny Barron contributed two songs... I contributed one song
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: At Davidson College of Rutgers University I was one of the jazz professors under Larry Ridley, with Ted Dunbar, Kenny Barron, and the late Freddie Waits. We had a quintet called the Jazz Professors.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: I threw her in with like real guys: Kenny Barron, Ron Carter, Lewis Nash, Fathead, Hank Crawford. I said look, if I’m going to make a record with her I want to make it with my guys
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: I knew Kenny was in Philadelphia, and we needed a piano player – the Jazztet. I told Art [Farmer] about Kenny, and Kenny came over and joined the group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Max Fleming
about_quotes.evidence: And for me the greatest thing that’s ever happened to me is that I’ve been able to play with the greats. Bill Evans. Kenny Barron.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Max Fleming interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, June 8, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Yusef Lateef
about_quotes.evidence: the group at that time, we did a book called Something Else, with Kenneth Barron, Albert Heath, Bob Cunningham, myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Yusef Lateef — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
